rulings.Case Title: THE AVINOAM MANDELBROT FAMILY TRUST DATED JULY 19, 2005
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Alternative Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing required. Parties to appear, prepared to address the following: 1. The procedural effect of the Appellate Court's October 12, 20222 Dismissal Order, dismissing the petition for writ of mandate; 2. This court's reconsideration of Trustee Sobol's requests for the court to either 1) order Trustee Sobol to post a bond as a condition for expunging the lis pendens filed against 2533 Moraga Street, or 2) require the Petitioners post a bond to maintain the lis pendens filed against 2533 Moraga Street; 3. The Appellate Court's "preliminary conclusion" stated in the September 12, 2022 Order Issuing Alternative Writ of Mandate that the properties titled to legal entities (i.e. 2640-2644-2650 Mission Street, San Francisco, 1155 Evans Drive, Santa Rosa, 7488 Bridget Drive, Rohnert park, 510-512 Todd Rd, Santa Rosa) are not properly the subject of Petitioners' lis pendens, with citation to Pacific Lumber Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1990) 226 Cal.App.3d 371. How should the court proceed as to those properties, in light of the Appellate Court's preliminary conclusion and case citation?; 4. Here, Petitioners request the court issue interim orders pursuant to the court's powers under Probate Code section 17206, specifically to prohibit the sale of the "subject properties" while the trust contest is pending. Petitioners' Petition Concerning Internal Affair of the Trust and for Financial Elder Abuse, brought under Prob. Code section 17200 and Welf. & Inst. Code sections 15600 et seq., is currently set for hearing on November 14, 2022. Petitioners' Petition for Interim Relief Prohibiting Respondent from Using Trust Assets to Defend Contest; Requiring Her Immediately to Reimburse the Trust for All Prior Disbursements with Interest; Suspending Respondent as Trustee; and Appointing a Private Professional Fiduciary to Manage the Trust Under Court Supervision Pending Resolution of the Litigation, brought under Prob. Code sections 17200, 17206, and 15642(e), is currently set to be heard January 3, 2023. How should the court proceed to adequately preserve the status quo and protect all interested parties' rights, pending resolution of the underlying legal disputes?
